name: Alex Morgan
title: Ph.D. Candidate in Computational Social Science
affiliation: Department of Information Science, Cornell University
bio: I am a Ph.D. candidate in Computational Social Science at Cornell University. My research sits at the intersection of human behavior, digital platforms, and data-driven methods — with a focus on how people navigate information environments and make decisions online.

I combine large-scale observational studies, online experiments, and machine learning to understand how platform design and algorithmic curation shape what people believe and how they act. My current work focuses on trust, misinformation, and the behavioral effects of recommendation systems.
Before Cornell, I worked as a data scientist at a civic tech nonprofit and completed a B.S. in Applied Mathematics at the University of Michigan.
